subUnits.title: 12.1 Comprehensive Revision of Third Term Physics (Circular Motion & SHM)
notes: Comprehensive Revision: Circular Motion & Simple Harmonic Motion
- Angular velocity: ω = v/r, ω = θ/t
- Applications: vehicle turning, washing machines, planetary orbits
- Energy: KE ↔ PE interchange, total energy constant
- Resonance: maximum amplitude at natural frequency match
- Forgetting direction of acceleration in SHM (always toward equilibrium)
- Mixing up period formulas for pendulum vs spring
- Solve past WAEC/NECO questions under timed conditions
- Align with WAEC/NECO practical and theory standards
- Emphasize local applications (okada turns, suspension systems, pendulum clocks)
- Master circular motion and SHM formulas and concepts
- Practice past questions under exam conditions
- Focus on units, direction, and real-life applications
- Improve problem-solving speed and accuracy for exams
diagramDescriptions: Chart of key formulas for circular motion and SHM
Diagram of common exam pitfalls and corrections
Sample past questions with step-by-step solutions
Nigerian context: Real-life applications and exam standards
Common misconceptions and how to avoid them
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SSS1 Physics circular motion SHM revision WAEC NECO site:youtube.com

quiz.explanation: Centripetal force is always directed inward toward the center of the circular path.

quiz.q: The period of a simple pendulum depends on:
opts: Length and acceleration due to gravity
quiz.explanation: Pendulum period depends only on length and g, not mass or amplitude (for small angles).

quiz.q: In SHM, acceleration is maximum at:
quiz.explanation: Acceleration is maximum at extremes where displacement is maximum (a = -ω²x).
Resonance occurs when driving frequency matches the natural frequency of the system.

opts: Confusing centripetal and centrifugal force direction
quiz.explanation: Students often confuse centripetal (inward) with centrifugal (apparent outward) force direction.

quiz.q: To improve exam performance, you should:
opts: Practice past questions under timed conditions
quiz.explanation: Timed past question practice builds familiarity, speed, and accuracy.

quiz.q: The total energy in SHM is:
quiz.explanation: Total mechanical energy is constant, continuously converting between kinetic and potential energy.

quiz.q: A common misconception about SHM is:
quiz.explanation: Velocity is maximum at equilibrium, not zero.

quiz.q: When solving circular motion problems, you should:
opts: Identify the source of centripetal force
quiz.explanation: Identifying the source of centripetal force (friction, tension, gravity) is essential for solving problems.

quiz.q: In revision, you should focus on:
opts: Formulas, past questions, units, and real-life applications
quiz.explanation: Comprehensive revision requires formulas, past questions, unit consistency, and application understanding.
